stimulus.content: Norway is located far north in Europe, yet its western coast has milder winters than other places at the same latitude. Scientists explain that a warm ocean current called the North Atlantic Current carries heat from the tropics northward along the coast.

stem: According to the passage, why does western Norway have milder winters than expected?

choices.text: A warm ocean current brings heat to the coast
Mountains block cold winds from reaching Norway
Norway is closer to the equator than it appears
Thick forests trap heat along the western coast

answer_explanation: The passage states that the North Atlantic Current carries warm water from the tropics to Norway's western coast, making winters milder than other places at the same latitude.
